title: Number of Islands
description: Given an m x n 2D binary grid grid which represents a map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
hints: Use DFS to explore all cells connected to a land cell
Mark visited cells by changing '1' to '0' or using a visited set
Iterate through each cell and start DFS when a land cell is found

title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy of the graph. Each node contains a value and a list of its neighbors.
hints: Use a hash map to store cloned nodes to avoid revisiting
DFS through the original graph and clone each node and its neighbors
Handle the empty graph case

title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
description: Given an m x n matrix of integers heights where heights[r][c] represents the height at cell (r, c), return a list of coordinates where water can flow to both the Pacific and Atlantic oceans. Water can flow from a cell to an adjacent cell with height less than or equal to its current height.
hints: Start DFS from ocean borders rather than each cell
Find cells reachable from Pacific (top and left borders) and Atlantic (bottom and right borders) separately
Water flows to adjacent cells with height >= current cell height when going from ocean inward

title: Max Area of Island
description: Given an m x n binary matrix grid, return the maximum area of an island in the grid. An island is a group of 1s connected 4-directionally. The area of an island is the number of cells with value 1 in the island.
hints: Use DFS to count the area of each island
Mark visited cells by changing 1 to 0
Track the maximum area found across all islands

title: Island Perimeter
description: Given an m x n binary matrix grid where 1 represents land and 0 represents water, calculate the perimeter of the island. The grid contains exactly one island and there are no lakes.
hints: Each land cell contributes 4 to the perimeter minus shared edges with adjacent land cells
For each land cell, check all 4 directions and subtract 1 for each adjacent land cell
Alternatively use DFS and count edges that reach water or grid boundary

title: Number of Provinces
description: Given an n x n matrix isConnected where isConnected[i][j] = 1 if the ith city and the jth city are directly connected and isConnected[i][j] = 0 otherwise. Return the number of provinces (connected components).
hints: Use DFS to traverse connected cities
Maintain a visited array to track which cities have been explored
Each unvisited city starts a new province

title: Number of Enclaves
description: Given an m x n binary matrix grid where 1 represents land and 0 represents water, return the number of land cells that cannot reach the boundary of the grid. You start from any land cell and can move 4-directionally to other land cells.
hints: Start DFS from all boundary land cells to mark reachable land as safe
Count remaining unmarked land cells after DFS from all borders
Land cells on the boundary and reachable from boundary are not enclaves

title: Number of Distinct Islands
description: Given a non-empty 2D array grid of 0s and 1s, an island is a group of 1s connected 4-directionally. Two islands are considered distinct if one island shape cannot be translated to match the other. Return the number of distinct islands.
hints: Use DFS to explore each island and record the path shape
Normalize the path by subtracting the starting position from all coordinates
Store normalized paths in a set to count distinct shapes

title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph
description: You have a graph of n nodes labeled from 0 to n - 1. You are given an integer n and an array edges where edges[i] = [ai, bi] indicates that there is an undirected edge between nodes ai and bi. Return the number of connected components in the graph.
hints: Build an adjacency list from the edge list
Use DFS to visit all nodes in each connected component
Count the number of times you start a new DFS from an unvisited node

title: Surrounded Regions
description: Given an m x n board containing 'X' and 'O', capture all regions surrounded by 'X'. A region is captured by flipping all 'O's into 'X's in that surrounded region. 'O's on the border and 'O's connected to border 'O's are not captured.
hints: Start DFS from all border 'O' cells to mark them as safe
After DFS, all remaining 'O' cells are surrounded and should be flipped
Convert safe cells back to 'O' and all other 'O' cells to 'X'
